title: AstraZeneca/Ionis Wainua Fails CARDIO-TTRansform in ATTR-CM
headline: AstraZeneca and Ionis' Wainua (eplontersen) failed the Phase III CARDIO-TTRansform trial in ATTR-CM, a surprise setback that boosted rivals BridgeBio and Alnylam.
key_facts: Trial: CARDIO-TTRansform, Phase III, in transthyretin-mediated amyloid cardiomyopathy (ATTR-CM); drug: Wainua (eplontersen), an antisense drug already approved in the US for polyneuropathy of hereditary transthyretin-mediated amyloidosis (hATTR-PN); Ionis shares down ~20-21% and AstraZeneca down ~8-9% per researcher notes (NOT independently confirmed — source text only says shares 'slide'); failure 'caught Wall Street analysts by surprise'

title: BridgeBio Surges 17% on Rival's ATTR-CM Failure
headline: BridgeBio shares surged ~17% on July 9 as the AstraZeneca/Ionis ATTR-CM failure removed a competitor to its Attruby (acoramidis).
key_facts: BBIO +17.0% on July 9, 2026 = +$13.34, to $91.67 (GuruFocus); alternate figure +15.7% (QuiverQuant, intraday); from ~$68 close mid-June to ~$91.98; $1B capital raise last week ahead of three more drug launches; RESEARCHER CORRECTION — PROPEL 3 achondroplasia data (infigratinib) was February 2026, NOT July 9; the surge is driven by the Wainua failure, not fresh data

title: Roche Scraps Two Ionis-Partnered Huntington's Drugs
headline: Roche ended development of tominersen and an early-stage Huntington's asset after a decade of trials.
key_facts: tominersen (Ionis-partnered) plus one early-stage Huntington's asset discontinued after a decade of trials; second Ionis-negative story of the day, compounding the Wainua failure

title: Ipsen Dysport Posts Dual Phase 3 Wins in Migraine
headline: Ipsen's Botox rival Dysport posted dual Phase 3 wins in migraine, positioning it to challenge AbbVie's Botox.
key_facts: Two Phase 3 trials succeeded (dual wins) in migraine; Dysport is a botulinum toxin; competitor is Botox (AbbVie/Allergan)

title: HHS Proposes COVID Vaccine Injury Table
headline: HHS proposed creating a table of COVID vaccine injuries to speed payout of injury claims.
key_facts: No numbers provided in source; HHS advances plan to pay out COVID-19 vaccine injury claims; policy/Trump administration

title: BioNTech Lays Commercial Groundwork for PD-L1xVEGF Bispecific
headline: BioNTech is building commercial infrastructure for its PD-L1xVEGF bispecific, using a HER2 ADC as the beachhead.
key_facts: PD-L1xVEGF bispecific commercial groundwork via a HER2 ADC; PD-L1xVEGF is a hot class, cf. Pfizer's VEGF-bispecific atirmociclib

title: UK NICE Recommends Against Amgen's Lumakras in Lung Cancer
headline: The UK's NICE price watchdog recommended against Amgen's Lumakras in lung cancer.
key_facts: NICE recommended against Lumakras (sotorasib, a KRAS inhibitor) in lung cancer; ex-US; KRAS thread relevance

title: GSK Ends Alector Immuno-Neurology Alliance
headline: GSK walked away from its immuno-neurology pact with Alector as the bet fizzled.
key_facts: GSK ends brain drug alliance with Alector; immuno-neurology bet fizzled

title: Tarsus Drops on Short-Seller Copay Allegation
headline: Tarsus stock dropped after short-seller Culper alleged an illegal copay scheme and called 2026 profit estimates wildly inflated.
key_facts: Short-seller Culper alleges illegal copay scheme; claims addressable market is a fraction of guidance; 2026 profit estimates 'wildly inflated'; listed under SECONDARY/CONTEXT ITEMS (not July 9 primary)

title: Pfizer HSBC Downgrade — Growth Rests on VEGF-Bispecific
headline: HSBC downgraded Pfizer on July 6, cutting its price target from $32 to $28, with growth now pinned on atirmociclib.
key_facts: HSBC downgrade July 6, 2026; PT cut $32 to $28; beta raised 0.78 to 0.85; growth depends on atirmociclib, a VEGF-bispecific; listed under SECONDARY/CONTEXT ITEMS (not July 9 primary)

title: Vertex/Crinetics $10B Acquisition (Context)
headline: Vertex's $10B acquisition of Crinetics, announced around July 6, with two drugs that could bring more than $5B annually.
key_facts: $10B acquisition closed/announced ~Jul 6; two drugs could bring >$5B annually; listed under SECONDARY/CONTEXT ITEMS (not July 9 primary)

title: Novartis to Acquire ADC Developer for Up to $1.5B (Context)
headline: Novartis is acquiring an ADC developer in a deal worth up to $1.5 billion, covering two ADCs.
key_facts: Deal worth up to $1.5B; two ADCs; listed under SECONDARY/CONTEXT ITEMS (not July 9 primary)

title: Vera Therapeutics Wins FDA Nod in IgA Nephropathy
headline: Vera Therapeutics secured an FDA nod for its IgA nephropathy drug, setting up competition with Otsuka.
key_facts: FDA nod for IgA nephropathy drug; competes with Otsuka; listed under SECONDARY/CONTEXT ITEMS (not July 9 primary)

title: Ipsen Acquires Memo Therapeutics for Up to $1.75B
headline: Ipsen acquired Memo Therapeutics for up to $1.75 billion for potravitug, a BK polyomavirus antibody, around July 1.
key_facts: Acquisition up to $1.75B for potravitug (BK polyomavirus antibody), ~Jul 1; listed under SECONDARY/CONTEXT ITEMS (not July 9 primary)

title: FDA Paused Public Release of Rejection Letters
headline: The FDA paused public release of complete response letters after an April citizen petition; the last published CRL was AbbVie's botox shot on April 22.
key_facts: FDA paused public CRL release after April citizen petition; last published CRL = AbbVie botox shot, April 22; listed under SECONDARY/CONTEXT ITEMS (not July 9 primary)
